name: United States
intro: In the United States, physical gold is classified as a collectible, resulting in a federal tax rate on the gain of up to 28%, distinct from usual stock rates.
investmentGoldText: The United States has no federal VAT, so there's no equivalent legal category of "investment gold." What matters instead is how the IRS classifies physical gold for tax purposes: bars, rounds, and coins are treated as a "collectible" under IRC §408(m), regardless of purity, which is why the capital-gains treatment described below differs from ordinary stocks. Separately, if gold is held inside a retirement account, only bars and coins meeting specific fineness and refiner requirements — generally .995 fine or better, plus a short list of named coins such as the American Gold Eagle — are IRA-eligible under §408(m)(3). That eligibility rule affects account structuring, not the resale tax treatment covered here.
vatText: The United States levies no federal VAT. Gold imported from Switzerland is generally free of customs duty, but its value must be declared to U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) once it reaches the reporting threshold below. Some states apply their own sales tax depending on the type of transaction and the buyer's residence.
customsIntro: Physical gold is not a "monetary instrument" for FinCEN Form 105 purposes, but its value must still be declared to CBP once it reaches $10,000.

customsRows.requirement: Mandatory value declaration to CBP; FinCEN Form 105 may also be requested

resaleIntro: In the U.S., physical gold is treated as a "collectible" rather than a standard capital asset, which caps the federal long-term rate at 28% instead of the lower rates that apply to stocks.

resaleRows.treatment: Taxed as a collectible at your capital gains rate, capped at 28% federally
Taxed as ordinary income at your marginal federal rate
Many states add their own capital gains or income tax on top of the federal rate — check your state of residence

faqs.q: Is physical gold treated like a stock for tax purposes?
faqs.a: No, the IRS classifies it as a collectible, with a maximum federal rate of 28% on long-term gain instead of standard capital gains rates.

faqs.q: Is there a federal VAT on gold in the US?
faqs.a: No, but some states apply a sales tax depending on the transaction.
Yes. Gold held over one year gets long-term collectible treatment (up to 28% federal); gold held under one year is taxed as ordinary income at your marginal rate.
